acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A hybrid canonical-remote / legacy-local migration test asserts remote-parent selection and `--no-push` origin isolation.
- [ ] Divergence-path migration tests assert merged metadata behavior in `meta.json`.
- [ ] Divergence tests would fail if remote-first `root_slug` selection or max-stamp preservation regressed.
description:
**Problem**
The divergence-path migration coverage still leaves two important branches under-asserted. There is no realistic test for the hybrid case where `origin` is already canonical and the local store is the only side that still needs rewriting, so parent selection and `--no-push` origin isolation in that branch are not pinned. The related/unrelated divergence tests also do not inspect `meta.json`, so dual-sided metadata selection rules can still regress silently.

**Files**: crates/beads-git/src/sync.rs, crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/cli/migration.rs, docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md
design:
**Design**
Expand divergence coverage around parent selection, origin isolation, and metadata merge rules.

1. Add a hybrid canonical-remote / legacy-local test that proves rewrite commits parent on the remote head and keep origin untouched under `--no-push`.
2. Extend divergence tests to seed both local and remote metadata and assert the merged `root_slug`, `last_write_stamp`, and recomputed checksums in the rewritten snapshot.
3. Keep the assertions tied to the design doc’s remote-first / max-stamp metadata policy.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 4cd04383: migration coverage now exercises the hybrid origin-canonical/local-legacy branch, verifies --no-push origin isolation, pins parent selection against the remote winner, and asserts merged metadata plus checksums in related-divergence cases. --repo /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs

title: Migration follow-up: close remaining divergence coverage gaps for parenting, origin isolation, and merged meta

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `list` gains explicit higher-value UX affordances from the comparison, such as `--all` and clearer output-mode control.
- [ ] Added filters or shortcuts are documented clearly and tested.
- [ ] The resulting command is easier to use for triage without becoming incoherent.
description:
**Problem**
beads-rs `list` is serviceable but notably behind beads-go for work discovery UX. beads-go offers clearer output modes and richer shortcuts such as `--all`, tree/flat modes, watch/pretty behavior, and selected higher-signal filters that make triage and navigation faster.

**Files:** crates/beads-cli/src/commands/list.rs, crates/beads-cli/src/filters.rs, related render/tests
design: Port the list affordances that materially improve work discovery without overloading the default surface. Prioritize explicit output mode control and a handful of useful shortcuts/filters over mirroring every flag beads-go has.
title: Port high-value work-discovery UX to bd list
